EXO-01.writer_recommended_label: The "Enabled" preset turns on all three MailTips features (general tips, external-recipient warnings, large-audience warnings) and sets the large-audience threshold to 25 — matching CIS guidance.

EXO-02.writer_recommended_label: Disabling auto-forward on the Default remote domain blocks the most common BEC exfiltration vector. Non-Default remote domains (deliberate partner federations) are unaffected.

writer_options.auto_forward_enabled_on_default_domain_b: Auto-forward enabled on Default domain (BEC exfiltration vector)

EXO-03.writer_recommended_label: Mailbox auditing is the prerequisite for every email-based forensic investigation. Microsoft enables it by default on new mailboxes since 2019, but legacy / migrated mailboxes may have it off. Apply syncs every user mailbox to AuditEnabled = $true.

writer_options.disabled_no_per_mailbox_audit_logging_fo: Disabled — no per-mailbox audit logging (forensics gap)

EXO-05.writer_recommended_label: The "Block + alert MSP" preset stops compromised accounts from sending more outbound mail (24h auto-release) AND alerts the MSP recipient list so operators can investigate. Microsoft default is alert-only, which does NOT block the spam.

writer_options.block_compromised_accounts_24h_alert_msp: Block compromised accounts (24h) + alert MSP recipients (recommended)
writer_options.microsoft_default_alert_only_does_not_bl: Microsoft default (Alert-only — does NOT block compromised accounts)

EXO-06.writer_recommended_label: Standard alone is the floor every tenant should have — turning it on tenant-wide is the single highest-value EXO hardening step. Standard + Strict adds defense in depth for tenants with high-value targets (executives, finance, legal). Disabled is "default Microsoft policies" which Microsoft acknowledges is loose.

writer_options.standard_strict_defense_in_depth_strict_: Standard + Strict (defense in depth — Strict for high-risk users on top of Standard baseline)
writer_options.no_presets_tenant_runs_on_default_polici: No presets — tenant runs on default policies (Microsoft acknowledges these are loose)

EXO-08.writer_recommended_label: Setting this to "Disabled" turns off basic-auth SMTP submission tenant-wide. Per-mailbox overrides are still possible for specific service accounts that genuinely need SMTP AUTH.

writer_options.smtp_auth_enabled_tenant_wide_basic_auth: SMTP AUTH enabled tenant-wide (basic-auth credential-stuffing exposure)

ENT-01.writer_recommended_label: The "Standard SSPR config" enables Microsoft Authenticator (preferred), SMS (fallback), and Email (recovery) for all users — Microsoft's recommended SSPR baseline. Optional advanced methods (FIDO2, TAP, OATH tokens, etc.) can be enabled via the "Additional Authentication Methods" expandable section below.

writer_options.standard_sspr_config_authenticator_sms_e: Standard SSPR config — Authenticator + SMS + Email enabled for all users (recommended)

ENT-05.writer_recommended_label: The recommended configuration enables both app-name and location-context displays alongside Microsoft's tenant-default number matching. The two extras make prompt-bombing attacks meaningfully harder by forcing the user to look at the sign-in screen.

writer_options.number_matching_app_and_location_context: Number matching + app and location context (recommended)
writer_options.number_matching_only_no_extra_context_di: Number matching only — no extra context displayed

ENT-06.writer_recommended_label: Custom banned words catch the company-specific patterns Microsoft's global list won't — your company name, product names, local geography, founder/exec names. 8-12 terms is the sweet spot. Anything longer has diminishing returns.

writer_options.global_microsoft_list_tenant_specific_cu: Global Microsoft list + tenant-specific custom banned words (recommended)
writer_options.global_microsoft_list_only_no_tenant_spe: Global Microsoft list only (no tenant-specific words)

ENT-07.writer_recommended_label: Microsoft now recommends "Let Microsoft manage your consent settings" — they auto-update the underlying policy as their guidance evolves. Choose "Do not allow user consent" only if you have an admin-consent request workflow set up and monitored.

writer_options.let_microsoft_manage_your_consent_settin: Let Microsoft manage your consent settings (recommended)
writer_options.do_not_allow_user_consent_admin_consent_: Do not allow user consent (admin-consent workflow required)
writer_options.allow_user_consent_for_verified_publishe: Allow user consent for verified publishers, low-impact permissions only

ENT-09.writer_recommended_label: Microsoft recommends Restricted Guest User for tenants that do not need guests browsing the directory.

SPO-01.writer_recommended_label: Microsoft recommends "Existing guests only" for SMB tenants that share with named partners but never with anonymous users.

SPO-02.writer_recommended_label: Microsoft recommends restricting the OneDrive sync app on unmanaged devices for any tenant that has Conditional Access available.

TEA-01.writer_recommended_label: There is NO universal recommendation — choose based on the tenant's external-meeting profile. Tenants that only meet with M365 federated partners → Hardened. Tenants running training, coaching, sales, or other meetings with non-business external attendees → Permissive with lobby. Open mode is genuinely dangerous and should never be deliberately chosen.

writer_options.hardened_anonymous_join_blocked_lobby_ho: Hardened — anonymous join blocked + lobby holds external (federated-only meeting tenants)
writer_options.permissive_with_lobby_anonymous_join_all: Permissive with lobby — anonymous join allowed + lobby holds external (training, coaching, sales meetings)
writer_options.open_anonymous_join_allowed_everyone_aut: Open — anonymous join allowed + everyone auto-admits (Zoom-bombing risk)

TEA-02.writer_recommended_label: Most SMBs want "Open + consumer blocked" — federation works with any M365 tenant for business comms, but personal Microsoft accounts cannot chat your users. Allowlist mode is the most secure but requires per-partner curation. Federation OFF is the most restrictive and may be disruptive.

writer_options.open_to_other_businesses_personal_accoun: Open to other businesses, personal accounts blocked (recommended for most SMBs)
writer_options.federation_restricted_to_specific_partne: Federation restricted to specific partner domains (most secure when business needs allow)
writer_options.federation_disabled_no_external_teams_ch: Federation disabled — no external Teams chat (most restrictive)
writer_options.open_personal_microsoft_accounts_allowed: Open + personal Microsoft accounts allowed (Microsoft default — risky)

CMP-01.writer_recommended_label: Enable the Unified Audit Log on every tenant — without it, no security incident can be investigated. Microsoft typically enables this by default on new tenants, but older tenants sometimes need an explicit Apply.
